items.headline: Ronaldo's dream lives on — Portugal edge Croatia 2-1 in a VAR thriller
items.body: The last dance for the 40-somethings had everything. Ivan Perišić put Croatia ahead early in the second half before Cristiano Ronaldo buried a penalty to level it — his first-ever World Cup knockout goal. Then, deep in stoppage time, sub Gonçalo Ramos headed home a Rafael Leão cross for the winner. Croatia thought they'd equalized moments later, but VAR chalked it off for offside — sending Luka Modrić home in what's almost certainly his final World Cup. Portugal get Spain next in Dallas on Monday.
items.say: Portugal beat Croatia 2-1 last night — Ronaldo scored a penalty and Gonçalo Ramos won it in stoppage time, then VAR wiped out Croatia's equalizer. Brutal way for Modrić to bow out. Spain next.

links.text: NBC Sports: Portugal 2-1 Croatia, Modrić's farewell

items.headline: Messi and Argentina begin their knockout run tonight vs. debutants Cape Verde
items.body: The defending champs close out the Round of 32 tonight in Miami (6pm ET), and it's Messi's stage — he's already the World Cup's all-time leading scorer and tops this tournament with six goals, including a hat-trick against Algeria. Standing in the way: Cape Verde, the smallest nation ever to reach a World Cup knockout, who snuck through with three gritty draws (including a 0-0 against Spain). Win and Argentina roll into the last 16 — where the bracket really heats up with France-Paraguay kicking off Saturday.
items.say: Messi and Argentina play their World Cup knockout tonight against Cape Verde — this tiny island nation in their first-ever World Cup. Should be a fun one. You watching?

links.text: Al Jazeera: Argentina vs. Cape Verde preview
FOX Sports: how to watch Messi vs. Cape Verde

items.headline: Naomi Osaka is stealing the show at Wimbledon — on and off the court
items.body: Osaka is rolling through the grass, cruising past Anastasia Gasanova 6-3, 6-2 to reach the third round, and she's back on Centre Court today. But she's turning as many heads with her walk-on wardrobe: a series of custom kimono-inspired looks (complete with a traditional kanzashi hair ornament) by Tokyo designer Hana Yagi, a nod to her Japanese roots. Style and substance — the four-time Slam champ looks like she's rediscovering her old form.
items.say: Naomi Osaka's having a moment at Wimbledon — she's winning easily and turning up in these gorgeous custom kimono outfits for her walk-ons. Both the tennis and the fits are worth a look.

links.text: Olympics.com: Osaka cruises into the third round

items.headline: The Esports World Cup — gaming's biggest event ever — kicks off this week
items.body: Think Olympics, but for video games. The Esports World Cup runs July 6–Aug 23 with a record prize pool north of $70M spread across 25 tournaments in 24 games — Counter-Strike, League, Valorant, Dota, the works. It's in Paris this year (relocated from Riyadh), the Valorant groups are already live, and the opening ceremony on July 8 has DJ Snake and Aya Nakamura. Even Cristiano Ronaldo signed on as global ambassador.
items.say: The Esports World Cup starts this week — it's basically the Olympics of gaming, like $70 million on the line across every big game. Ronaldo's even the ambassador. Wild event.

items.headline: The Yankees are in free-fall — seven straight losses and counting
items.body: What a slide. New York has dropped seven in a row heading into the holiday weekend — swept in Tampa by the surging Rays (now 3.5 games up in the AL East) and then swept by the Tigers, their first series sweep of the Yankees since 2008. With Aaron Judge sidelined by a rib injury, the bats have gone quiet, and the captain publicly called out the team's focus. A rough Fourth of July to be a Yankees fan.
items.say: The Yankees have lost seven in a row — got swept twice, and Judge is hurt. Meanwhile the Rays are red-hot and running away with the division. Rough week in the Bronx.

links.text: FOX Sports: Rays and Yankees going opposite ways

items.headline: Madonna is back on the dance floor — 'Confessions II' drops today
items.body: Twenty-one years after 'Confessions on a Dance Floor,' Madonna reunites with producer Stuart Price for a full-blown sequel. Her 15th album lands today with a stacked guest list — Sabrina Carpenter, Feid, Stromae, Martin Garrix and even her daughter Lola — and early reviews are calling it a 'magnificent return to the dance floor.' The lead single 'Bring Your Love,' a giddy duet with Carpenter, already topped dance charts.
items.say: Madonna dropped a whole sequel to her 'Confessions on a Dance Floor' album today — 21 years later, with Sabrina Carpenter on it. Critics are loving it. Perfect weekend party record.

links.text: 'Bring Your Love' ft. Sabrina Carpenter (official video)

items.headline: 'X-Men '97' is back — Season 2 landed with a three-episode drop
items.body: The show that stole 2024 returned to Disney+ this week, and it dropped three episodes at once to kick things off. This time the X-Men are scattered across time — from Ancient Egypt to the far future — scrambling to get home and stop Apocalypse. Nine episodes roll out weekly through mid-August, and fans say the animation and the emotional gut-punches are as good as ever.
items.say: X-Men '97 Season 2 is finally out on Disney+ — they dropped three episodes to start. If you loved the first season, clear your weekend. It's so good.

items.headline: Céline Dion's emotional comeback rolls on with a new single today
items.body: One of the great comeback stories in music keeps going. Still navigating stiff-person syndrome, Céline Dion released a new French single, 'Bonjour, Pardon, Merci,' today — following her first comeback song 'Dansons' in the spring. She's also announced a Paris residency for the fall that sold out almost instantly. After everything she's been through, hearing that voice again is something.
items.say: Céline Dion put out another new song today — she's slowly making her comeback after that rare illness she's been fighting. Honestly kind of emotional to hear her singing again.

links.text: CTV News: Dion unveils new song

items.headline: Joey Chestnut chases an 18th Mustard Belt at Nathan's tomorrow
items.body: The most American 12 minutes of the year is back. The Nathan's Hot Dog Eating Contest returns to Coney Island on the Fourth, with legend Joey 'Jaws' Chestnut going for an 18th title (his record is a frankly absurd 76 dogs and buns in 10 minutes). On the women's side, Miki Sudo is chasing a fifth straight crown. It's on ESPN/ABC around lunchtime — the perfect ridiculous background watch for your cookout.
items.say: Nathan's Hot Dog Eating Contest is tomorrow — Joey Chestnut going for his 18th win. The guy once ate 76 hot dogs in ten minutes. Peak Fourth of July TV, honestly.

links.text: Joey Chestnut's record-chasing eating (past contest, CBS Sports)
ESPN: how to watch the 2026 contest
